title: hypericum elatoides
intro: Hypericum elatoides, also known as St. John's Spire or Creeping St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant species in the St. John's Wort family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant contains bioactive compounds, including hyperforin and hypericin, which have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Hypericum elatoides include wound healing, pain relief, and antimicrobial activity, making it a popular herbal remedy for skin issues and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Hypericum elatoides include teas, tinctures, and salves that can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
